query: Rivian 2026 delivery guidance 62000 67000 vehicles percentage increase R2 launch second quarter
a_issues: A contains weaker sourcing and more ambiguity. It has the Rivian newsroom result and a Quartz summary with the raised guidance to 65,000-70,000 and Q2 deliveries, but it does not clearly surface the original February guidance context or the second-quarter launch timing as directly as B. It also includes lower-reliability or thin results (Facebook post snippet, cbtnews, eletric-vehicles.com). One result says the old 62,000-67,000 guidance was up 47% to 59%, which is plausible for the earlier guidance but could confuse the user because the query mixes the old range with percentage increase and Q2/R2 launch timing, while another result discusses the new raised range. Overall A is somewhat noisier and less complete.
b_issues: B includes one likely typo in the CNBC snippet ('11,0000' units) and also includes lower-reliability aggregation content (eletric-vehicles.com). However, its core facts are consistent across CNBC, Reuters, Rivian newsroom, and Seeking Alpha. No major factual contradiction is present.
reasoning: B better enables a correct answer because it directly provides all key facts the question is asking about from strong sources: the prior 2026 delivery guidance was 62,000-67,000 vehicles; after Q2 Rivian raised it to 65,000-70,000; Q2 deliveries were 12,194; and the R2 launch/deliveries began in the second quarter, with smaller initial volumes in Q2 and ramping in the second half. B also includes a result explicitly quantifying the percentage increase over 2025 deliveries (53.9% to 65.7%), which is highly relevant to the query. A has some of these facts, but less directly and with noisier sourcing, making it slightly less reliable for an LLM answering from search results alone.
